channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DOH Confirms No Hantavirus Cases Among Filipino Crew in Quarantined Cruise Ship
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) confirmed that none of the 38 Filipino crew members on a cruise ship quarantined in Cabo Verde have shown symptoms of hantavirus. According to DOH spokesperson Albert Do...

item.title: Final Fragment of 17th-Century Flemish Tapestry Recovered by Spanish Police
item.description: Spanish authorities have recovered the last missing piece of a 17th-century Flemish tapestry, titled 'Apotheosis of the Arts,' which had been stolen 42 years ago. The large tapestry, measuring 4 by 6....

item.title: Tanzanian Universities Introduce Online Course to Help Students Report GBV and Access Health Services
item.description: University students in Tanzania are now gaining more knowledge and confidence to handle gender-based violence (GBV) thanks to the Our Rights, Our Lives, Our Future (O3 Plus) project. The initiative pr...

item.title: Nestoil Rejects Alleged Debt Claims, Says Dividend Suspension Linked to CBN Directive
item.description: Nestoil Limited don clear say all those stories wey dey talk say some Nigerian banks stop to pay dividends because dem dey owe Nestoil na lie. The company yan say na First Bank of Nigeria and their Ch...

item.title: Dutch Art Detective Recovers Six Stolen Paintings from Medemblik Town Hall
item.description: Dutch art detective Arthur Brand, renowned for his ability to recover stolen artworks, has successfully retrieved six paintings recently stolen from the Medemblik town hall in the Netherlands. This fo...

item.title: ORDLO Residents Reflect on Life After Russia Recognizes Donetsk and Luhansk
item.description: Following Russia's official recognition of the Donetsk and Luhansk People’s Republics, residents of the conflict-affected regions shared their immediate reactions and concerns about daily life. Many, ...

item.title: Nova Scotia Student Publishes New Illustrated Children’s Book
item.description: A student from Nova Scotia has recently achieved a remarkable milestone by publishing an illustrated children's book titled 'Darcy and Dash'. The book combines creative storytelling with vibrant illus...

item.title: Documenting the Human Stories of the Russian-Ukrainian War Through Photography
item.description: Anastasiia Vlasova has been capturing key moments of the Russian-Ukrainian war since its onset, including the pseudo-referendum in Crimea, the MH17 crash site, and the LPR and DPR territories. Now cov...

item.title: ReactOS Combines Installation Media with New GUI Installer and Improved Storage Driver
item.description: ReactOS, the open-source Windows-compatible operating system, has consolidated its installation options into a single unified BootCD, replacing the previously separate installation and LiveCD images. ...

item.title: Arsenal’s Champions League Final Run Earns Them Over KSh 20 Billion
item.description: Arsenal have secured a massive financial boost after qualifying for the UEFA Champions League final following their 2-1 aggregate win over Atletico Madrid. The decisive moment came at the Emirates Sta...

item.title: Samajwadi Party ends brief pact with poll consultancy I‑PAC, Akhilesh Yadav cites financial constraints
item.description: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav has announced that his party has ended its association with the Indian Political Action Committee (I‑PAC), a prominent election‑management and political consultanc...

item.title: Rwanda and Tanzania Commit to Reducing Trade Barriers to Improve Cross-Border Transport
item.description: Truck drivers and logistics stakeholders in Rwanda have welcomed the recent commitment by Rwanda and Tanzania to remove trade barriers that have been slowing cross-border business. This follows Presid...

item.title: BJP Accuses TMC Supporters of Impersonating Party Workers Amid Post-Election Tensions
item.description: In the wake of the West Bengal Assembly elections held on May 4, 2026, tensions between the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) and the Trinamool Congress (TMC) have escalated. The BJP has alleged that TMC's...

item.title: Canadian Citizen Challenges U.S. DHS Subpoena for Google Data Over Online Criticism
item.description: A Canadian citizen, using the pseudonym John Doe, has filed a lawsuit in the U.S. District Court for Northern California to prevent the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) from obtaining his persona...

item.title: Joel Embiid out of Sixers vs Knicks game due to injury
item.description: The Philadelphia 76ers will face a significant challenge in their upcoming game against the New York Knicks as star center Joel Embiid is sidelined due to an injury. Embiid, who has been instrumental ...

item.title: Families of children with autism in South Africa face gaps in support, schooling and awareness
item.description: Parents raising children with autism in South Africa, particularly in provinces like the North West, continue to face serious challenges ranging from late diagnoses to limited access to specialised ed...

item.title: Life in Donbas Amid Escalating Tensions in February 2022
item.description: In February 2022, residents of the Donbas region in eastern Ukraine were preparing for war under uncertain conditions. Four documentary photographers reported for Bird in Flight on how life changed dr...

item.title: Canadian ETF Market Expands with First Space Fund and New Offerings from Major Providers
item.description: The Canadian ETF marketplace saw significant growth in April with the introduction of 32 new funds. RBC Global Asset Management launched seven ETFs, including actively managed equity funds like RBC Ca...

item.title: US and Iran reportedly close to preliminary peace memorandum to end Gulf conflict
item.description: Iran is reviewing a new US proposal as both countries edge closer to a preliminary one-page memorandum aimed at ending the ongoing war in the Gulf. The proposal, reportedly consisting of 14 points, wo...

item.title: Simba beats JKT Tanzania 1-0 to stay close to Yanga in league race
item.description: Simba SC managed to keep their title hopes alive with a tight 1-0 win over JKT Tanzania at the Major General Isamuhyo Stadium on May 6, 2026. The only goal of the game came in the 53rd minute when exp...
